paragraphs.title: Introduction
paragraphs.p: Please see the for the exam dates.

li.title: Exam 1 (13%)
li.p: Covers sections 3.9, 4.7, and 7.17.5. You will have the entire class period for this exam, but it will be written with the expecation that a few students will need less than one hour.

As you turn in your exam, you will be given the complete list of exercises to take home with you. You may redo one question and turn in your response the next class period to replace your original score for that question.

li.title: Exam 2 (13%)
li.p: Covers sections 8.18.4. You will have the entire class period for this exam, but it will be written with the expecation that a few students will need less than one hour.

As you turn in your exam, you will be given the complete list of exercises to take home with you. You may redo one question and turn in your response the next class period to replace your original score for that question.

li.title: Exam 3 (13%)
li.p: Covers sections 8.58.8. You will have the entire class period for this exam, but it will be written with the expecation that a few students will need less than one hour.
Unlike previous exams, there is no opportunity to make up one question. I will provide you with complete solutions to help you study for the final exam the following week.

li.title: Exam 4 (25%)
li.p: Covers everything from the course. The PCC math department requires a comprehensive final exam worth at least 25% of your final grade. It will be sufficient to be comfortable with the exercises from exams 1, 2, and 3.
You will have the entire 120 minutes of allotted final exam time (which goes from 9:0011:00).
Unlike previous exams, there is no opportunity to make up one question.

paragraphs.title: Missing an Exam
paragraphs.p: If you miss an exam unexpectedly, contact me to explain why as soon as is reasonable given whatever the curcumstances are, or I might not be able to allow a make-up and you would receive a zero.

paragraphs.title: Accommodated Testing
paragraphs.p: Students who experience disability-related barriers should contact PCC's AEDR office: . If students elect to use approved academic adjustments, they must provide, in advance, formal notification from AEDR to the instructor. Exam accommodations may include, but are not limited to, extended time and a distraction-free environment.
